#  Hostr

> semantic-release: angular

- [ Hostr](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/readme.md):  Hostr
- [AI](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/ai/readme.md): Hostr's AI surface is the MCP server that lets an agent search listings, manage a Hostr session, create or edit listings, negotiate reservations, handle payments, upload images, and inspect trips/bookings through the same SDK paths used by the app.
- [Infrastructure](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/infrastructure/readme.md): Infrastructure is split into two Terraform stacks:
- [App](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/app/readme.md): codecov
- [Escrow](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/escrow/readme.md): Hostr escrow is the settlement layer for reservations. It combines Nostr escrow-service events, on-chain EVM trade funding, payment-proof verification, and arbitration workflows exposed through the Hostr MCP server.

## AI

- [Hostr Daemon](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/ai/daemon/readme.md): The Hostr daemon is the Dart process behind the MCP server. It is not the public HTTP service. It is a local child process that the Node MCP bridge starts and talks to over stdio.

## Dependencies

### Nips

#### Accommodation nip

- [NIP-XX](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/dependencies/nips/accommodation-nip/xx.md): Accommodation Listing Extension for NIP-99

#### Escrow nip

- [NIP-XX](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/dependencies/nips/escrow-nip/xx.md): Escrow Services

#### Reservation nip

- [NIP-XX](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/dependencies/nips/reservation-nip/xx.md): Reservations

## Guides

- [Getting started](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/guides/getting_started.md): Install dependencies: dnsmasq and route hostr.development to docker.
- [Release Guide](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/guides/release.md): This document covers the full release lifecycle: branching, PRs, versioning, CI secrets, and deploying staging and production builds to the App Store and Play Store.

## Infrastructure

- [Tailscale](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/infrastructure/tailscale.md): The local dev stack uses Tailscale to make services running on your dev Mac (via Docker) accessible from other devices — primarily an iPhone for real-device testing.

## Source

### Ai

- [Hostr MCP Server](https://sudonym-btc.github.io/hostr/source/ai/mcp-server/readme.md): The Hostr MCP server exposes typed Hostr actions over MCP and delegates Hostr SDK work to the Dart hostr-daemon. The action catalog in hostr_cli/lib/src/actions/hostr_actions.dart is the source of truth for MCP tool names, input JSON schemas, generated TypeScript types, and workflow documentation.
